heading: Credit for producing oil and gas from marginal wells
label: 45I Credit for producing oil and gas from marginal wells

children.text: the credit amount, and
the qualified crude oil production and the qualified natural gas production which is attributable to the taxpayer.

children.label: A
children.text: $3 per barrel of qualified crude oil production, and

children.label: B
children.text: 50 cents per 1,000 cubic feet of qualified natural gas production.

children.heading: Reduction as oil and gas prices increase
children.label: 2 Reduction as oil and gas prices increase
i
children.text: the excess (if any) of the applicable reference price over $15 ($1.67 for qualified natural gas production), bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: $3 ($0.33 for qualified natural gas production).

children.heading: Inflation adjustment
children.label: B Inflation adjustment
children.text: In the case of any taxable year beginning in a calendar year after 2005, each of the dollar amounts contained in subparagraph (A) shall be increased to an amount equal to such dollar amount multiplied by the inflation adjustment factor for such calendar year (determined under section 43(b)(3)(B) by substituting “2004” for “1990”).

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of qualified crude oil production, the reference price determined under section 45K(d)(2)(C), and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of qualified natural gas production, the Secretary’s estimate of the annual average wellhead price per 1,000 cubic feet for all domestic natural gas.

children.heading: Qualified crude oil and natural gas production
children.label: c Qualified crude oil and natural gas production

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The terms “qualified crude oil production” and “qualified natural gas production” mean domestic crude oil or natural gas which is produced from a qualified marginal well.

children.heading: Limitation on amount of production which may qualify
children.label: 2 Limitation on amount of production which may qualify

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Crude oil or natural gas produced during any taxable year from any well shall not be treated as qualified crude oil production or qualified natural gas production to the extent production from the well during the taxable year exceeds 1,095 barrels or barrel-of-oil equivalents (as defined in section 45K(d)(5)).

children.heading: Short taxable years
children.label: i Short taxable years
children.text: In the case of a short taxable year, the limitations under this paragraph shall be proportionately reduced to reflect the ratio which the number of days in such taxable year bears to 365.

children.heading: Wells not in production entire year
children.label: ii Wells not in production entire year
children.text: In the case of a well which is not capable of production during each day of a taxable year, the limitations under this paragraph applicable to the well shall be proportionately reduced to reflect the ratio which the number of days of production bears to the total number of days in the taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: the production from which during the taxable year is treated as marginal production under section 613A(c)(6), or

children.label: I
children.text: has average daily production of not more than 25 barrel-of-oil equivalents (as so defined), and

children.label: II
children.text: produces water at a rate not less than 95 percent of total well effluent.

children.heading: Crude oil, etc.
children.label: B Crude oil, etc.
children.text: The terms “crude oil”, “natural gas”, “domestic”, and “barrel” have the meanings given such terms by section 613A(e).

children.heading: Production attributable to the taxpayer
children.label: 1 Production attributable to the taxpayer
children.text: In the case of a qualified marginal well in which there is more than one owner of operating interests in the well and the crude oil or natural gas production exceeds the limitation under subsection (c)(2), qualifying crude oil production or qualifying natural gas production attributable to the taxpayer shall be determined on the basis of the ratio which taxpayer’s revenue interest in the production bears to the aggregate of the revenue interests of all operating interest owners in the production.

children.heading: Operating interest required
children.label: 2 Operating interest required
children.text: Any credit under this section may be claimed only on production which is attributable to the holder of an operating interest.

children.heading: Production from nonconventional sources excluded
children.label: 3 Production from nonconventional sources excluded
children.text: In the case of production from a qualified marginal well which is eligible for the credit allowed under section 45K for the taxable year, no credit shall be allowable under this section unless the taxpayer elects not to claim the credit under section 45K with respect to the well.
